passage: Not a single member of the three research teams that participated in the multi-site study have been able to replicate the original findings under controlled conditions. The lead investigator has called for an independent replication effort using a standardized protocol.
question: Which choice best corrects the subject-verb agreement error in the first sentence?

choices.A: Not a single member of the three research teams that participated in the multi-site study has been able to replicate the original findings under controlled conditions.
choices.B: Not a single member of the three research teams that participated in the multi-site study have been able to replicate the original findings under controlled conditions.
choices.C: Not a single member of the three research teams that participated in the multi-site study were able to replicate the original findings under controlled conditions.
choices.D: No change; 'have been able' agrees with 'teams,' the nearest plural noun, which is the logical subject

explanation: The subject of the sentence is 'Not a single member,' which is singular—'member' is the head noun, and 'a single' indicates one individual. The prepositional phrase 'of the three research teams that participated in the multi-site study' is a modifier. The singular verb 'has been able' is required. Choice A correctly uses 'has been able.' Choice B preserves the error with 'have been able.' Choice C uses 'were able,' a past plural form—wrong in number. Choice D incorrectly identifies 'teams' as the logical subject; the grammatical and logical subject is 'member.'
